calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Russ Bolin, esq. (800-637-6922) of Law Offices of Steve Levy for Plaintiff via Court Call.
Christopher Woolsey (415-690-5502) Defendant in propria persona.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Defendant stated he knew about the debt, but didn't know he was being sued, and found out about the default judgment about a year ago. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER WOOLSEY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT pursuant to CCP 473.

Defendant Christopher Woolsey's motion to set aside default judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant's default was entered on March 11, 2015, and a default judgment was entered on March 26, 2015. A motion to set aside a default judgment brought under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b) must be filed within six months. (Code Civ. Proc. 473(b).) "The six-month limit is mandatory; a court has no authority to grant relief under section 473, subdivision (b), unless an application is made within the six-month period." (Arumbula v. Union Carbide Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 333, 340.) The Court has no authority to grant relief from the six-month limit, whether based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable or an attorney's affidavit of fault. (Id. at 345; accord, Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 918 ["The six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed."].) Alternatively, a motion to set aside a default judgment may be brought within two years under Section 473.5 if the moving party establishes that service has not resulted in actual notice in time to defend the action. (Code Civ. Proc. 473.5.) That provision does not apply. Contrary to Defendant's contention that he was never served with papers, the proof of service reflects that he was personally served with the summons and complaint on November 15, 2014, and Plaintiff's counsel's declaration establishes that Defendant was aware of the pendency of the action. In any event, the motion was not filed within two years of entry of the default judgment.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
